symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic progressive unilateral right leg swelling for ~5 years post-pelvic-surgery
symptoms.onset: insidious, ~5 years ago after pelvic surgery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary lymphoedema classically follows pelvic/nodal surgery and is chronic and progressive.
DVT is acute, not a 5-year chronic swelling.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constant heaviness and dull aching in the limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heaviness/aching is the typical symptom of a lymphoedematous limb.

symptoms.symptom_name: New sharply localised hot, red, tender patch on the shin (<24h)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute sharply demarcated hot tender erythema is the hallmark of cellulitis.
Redness can occur in dermatitis, but focal exquisite tenderness favours infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever / feeling warm (37.8 C self-measured), no rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic response (fever) accompanies bacterial cellulitis; absence of rigors argues against established sepsis for now.
Allergic dermatitis does not cause fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent prior cellulitis episodes in the same limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lymphoedematous limbs are prone to recurrent cellulitis; a history of prior episodes raises pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior pelvic surgery (cause of secondary lymphoedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nodal disruption from pelvic surgery is a classic cause of secondary lower-limb lymphoedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of prominent varicose veins / dependent rubor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of varicosities and venous skin changes makes a venous cause less likely and favours lymphatic aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-pitting, firm oedema from toes to groin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-pitting, whole-limb (toes-to-groin) oedema is characteristic of established lymphoedema; requires palpation and cannot be judged remotely.
DVT typically causes pitting, more diffuse and acute swelling rather than chronic non-pitting change.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Stemmer's sign (cannot pinch a skin fold at the base of the 2nd toe)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive Stemmer's sign is highly specific for lymphoedema; it is a clinician bedside test and cannot be performed via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperkeratotic, fibrotic skin with peau d'orange appearance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic dermal changes (hyperkeratosis, fibrosis, peau d'orange) reflect long-standing lymphatic congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: 10 x 8 cm sharply demarcated area of erythema, warmth and exquisite tenderness on the mid-shin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A measured, sharply demarcated hot tender plaque is the defining local sign of cellulitis; its exact margins guide the pen-marking used to monitor spread.

investigation_details.expected_result: Delayed lymphatic transit / reduced tracer clearance consistent with lymphoedema
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Not required acutely; it confirms the chronic lymphatic diagnosis in the outpatient setting and must NOT delay acute infection management.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Delayed transit confirms impaired lymphatic drainage; an outpatient confirmatory test, never available in real time in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden deep calf pain, no breathlessness, no chest pain (DVT/PE screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lowers but does not eliminate DVT probability — DVT still requires duplex ultrasound to exclude and cannot be ruled out on history.
Palpable pulses argue against an arterial cause of the painful limb; requires Doppler and cannot be assessed remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dusky/purple/black or blistering skin; pain proportionate to appearance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pain out of proportion, skin necrosis/blistering and systemic toxicity lowers necrotising-infection probability, but ongoing vigilance is required.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Non-pitting oedema + positive Stemmer's sign + hyperkeratotic skin + delayed lymphoscintigraphy transit together establish the chronic lymphatic diagnosis — but all four are clinician/investigation findings, sealed from the AI Doctor.
Acute hot tender demarcated plaque + fever + recurrent-infection history on a lymphoedematous limb is a high-probability cellulitis needing same-day antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous duplex (compression) ultrasound of the right leg
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The key not-to-miss step: cellulitis and DVT share the hot swollen painful leg presentation and DVT cannot be excluded on history or remotely. Same-day duplex ultrasound is required to exclude it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC and CRP (± blood cultures if systemically unwell)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Marks the severity of infection and provides a baseline; blood cultures if fever/systemic features develop. Also feeds a LRINEC calculation if necrotising infection is suspected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound/skin swab if any broken skin or discharge
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Targets antibiotic therapy if there is a portal of entry with exudate; not mandatory for intact-skin cellulitis.

Low value here — D-dimer is chronically elevated and non-specific in a chronically swollen, inflamed limb, and a positive result would not distinguish cellulitis from DVT. Proceed directly to duplex ultrasound instead.
Confirms the chronic lymphatic diagnosis but is an OUTPATIENT test — ordering it acutely adds nothing to managing the infection and must not delay antibiotics.
